input:
agent-browser - fast browser automation CLI for AI agents

Usage: agent-browser <command> [args] [options]

Core Commands:
  open <url>                 Navigate to URL
  click <sel>                Click element (or @ref)
  dblclick <sel>             Double-click element
  type <sel> <text>          Type into element
  fill <sel> <text>          Clear and fill
  press <key>                Press key (Enter, Tab, Control+a)
  keyboard type <text>       Type text with real keystrokes (no selector)
  keyboard inserttext <text> Insert text without key events
  hover <sel>                Hover element
  focus <sel>                Focus element
  check <sel>                Check checkbox
  uncheck <sel>              Uncheck checkbox
  select <sel> <val...>      Select dropdown option
  drag <src> <dst>           Drag and drop
  upload <sel> <files...>    Upload files
  download <sel> <path>      Download file by clicking element
  scroll <dir> [px]          Scroll (up/down/left/right)
  scrollintoview <sel>       Scroll element into view
  wait <sel|ms>              Wait for element or time
  screenshot [path]          Take screenshot
  pdf <path>                 Save as PDF
  snapshot
A: agent-browser: open, click, dblclick, type, fill, press, keyboard type, keyboard inserttext, hover, focus, check, uncheck, select, drag, upload, download, scroll, scrollintoview, wait, screenshot, pdf, snapshot, eval, connect.
B: agent-browser — fast browser automation CLI for AI agents. Commands: open <url>, click, dblclick, type, fill, press, keyboard type, keyboard inserttext, hover, focus, check, uncheck, select, drag, upload, download, scroll, scrollintoview, wait, screenshot, pdf, snapshot (accessibility tree with refs), eval, connect (CDP).

206	def register_ops_monitor_trigger() -> dict[str, Any] | None:
207	    """Register ops.monitor as a durable RemoteTrigger.
208	
209	    Creates or updates the RemoteTrigger in the Claude API and updates
210	    the local .claude/scheduled_tasks.json file with the trigger info.
211	    Returns the trigger info dict, or None if registration fails.
212	    """
213	    trigger_id = "3a7c9d1f"  # Fixed ID for ops.monitor
214	
215	    # Construct the callback URL pointing to the HTTP endpoint that invokes run_tick()
216	    callback_base_url = os.environ.get("SOLO_CALLBACK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8765")
217	    callback_url = f"{callback_base_url}/api/ops/trigger-cron-block"
218	
219	    # Create the RemoteTrigger via the API
220	    body = {
221	        "name": "ops.monitor",
222	        "cron": "*/15 * * * *",
223	        "recurring": True,
224	        "durable": True,
225	        "description": "HTTP health probe runner for ops monitoring",
226	        "callback_url": callback_url,
227	        "prompt": "When this trigger fires on the cron schedule, POST to /api/ops/trigger-cron-block with block_id='ops.monitor' and include the trigger authentication headers from the trigger re
A: register_ops_monitor_trigger() -> dict[str, Any] | None: fixed trigger_id "3a7c9d1f"; callback_url = SOLO_CALLBACK_BASE_URL (default "http://localhost:8765") + /api/ops/trigger-cron-block. Body: name "ops.monitor", cron "*/15 * * * *", recurring True, durable True, description "HTTP health probe runner for ops monitoring", prompt directing POST with block_id='ops.monitor' plus auth headers. Tries remote_trigger_call("get") then "update"; on failure "create"; returned id read from response "id" or "trigger_id"; API failures only log warning "Failed to register ops.monitor RemoteTrigger with API: {api_exc}". Always updates ~/.claude/scheduled_tasks.json regardless: migrates legacy "scheduled_tasks" array to dict keyed by block_id; writes entry with block_id "ops.monitor", trigger_id, cron, recurring, durable, callback_url, created_at (utcnow isoformat + "Z"); returns the entry, or None on write failure (warning logged). Next def at line 304: register_ops_error_scan_trigger().
B:
register_ops_monitor_trigger(): trigger_id "3a7c9d1f", callback_base_url "http://localhost:8765" (default), callback_url "http://localhost:8765/api/ops/trigger-cron-block", name "ops.monitor", cron "*/15 * * * *", recurring True, durable True, description "HTTP health probe runner for ops monitoring", prompt "When this trigger fires on the cron schedule, POST to /api/ops/trigger-cron-block with block_id='ops.monitor' and include the trigger authentication headers from the trigger registration."; update via remote_trigger_call("get", trigger_id=trigger_id) then remote_trigger_call("update", trigger_id=trigger_id, body=body), fallback remote_trigger_call("create", body=body); returns dict with trigger_id "3a7c9d1f" (or id from response), callback_url "http://localhost:8765/api/ops/trigger-cron-block", created_at "1970-01-01T00:00:00.000000Z" (default), written to .claude/scheduled_tasks.json; fails with warning "Failed to register ops.monitor RemoteTrigger with API" or "Failed to register ops.monitor RemoteTrigger: {exc}".

register_ops_error_scan_trigger() (skipped).

---

## COMPREHENSIVE EVIDENCE ANALYSIS: Steps 1-5 Verification

Based on my examination of all evidence directories from the completed steps, here's what has been built and verified:

### **STEP 1: Initial Trigger Registration & HTTP Endpoint (175 tests PASSED)**

**What Was Verified:**
- All 4 ops triggers (heartbeat, monitor, error_scan, metrics_review) registered at module init
- RemoteTrigger dataclass with metrics tracking (last_run, fire_count)
- HTTP endpoint `/api/ops/trigger-cron-block` accepts POST requests with trigger_id
- Trigger authentication via trigger_id lookup in scheduled_tasks.json
- OAuth token bypass for public endpoints (SOLO_OAUTH_TOKEN environment variable)
- Heartbeat block execution via subprocess invocation
- HEARTBEAT.md state file creation with operational metrics (pending_gates, task_transitions)
- Daemon integration with cron scheduling (daemon discovers and fires blocks)
- Error scan and metrics review triggers registered and firing
- Fire count increments persisted across daemon restarts

**Evidence Files:** c1.txt shows all 175 tests passing including criti
A:
Evidence analysis, all 5 steps verified:
Step 1 (175 tests PASSED): 4 ops triggers registered at module init; RemoteTrigger dataclass with last_run, fire_count; POST /api/ops/trigger-cron-block authenticated via trigger_id in scheduled_tasks.json; OAuth bypass via SOLO_OAUTH_TOKEN; heartbeat subprocess writes HEARTBEAT.md; daemon discovers and fires blocks; fire counts persist across restarts. Key passes: test_trigger_cron_block_fires_heartbeat, test_trigger_history_persistence_across_restart, test_daemon_fires_heartbeat_on_schedule.
Step 2 (25 tests PASSED): health checks handle success, timeout, 404, connection errors; callback URL from SOLO_CALLBACK_BASE_URL; app_health omitted when URL missing; nested health rendered in HEARTBEAT.md; real HTTP server tests (test_real_http_server_returns_health and others); consolidated heartbeat across businesses; onCron manifest validation.
Step 3 (18 + 69 tests PASSED): fire history to ~/.solo/callback_executions.jsonl (trigger_id, fire_count, timestamp, duration, exit_code); legacy array format migrated to dict-keyed; four registration functions consolidated into parametric register_ops_trigger(); SOLO_TARGET validation; monitor activation fixed onDemand to onCron; metrics review consolidation deduplicated in solo_cron.py.
Step 4 (6 acceptance checks PASSED): RemoteTriggerCallbackInvoker with exponential backoff, max_retries default 3, logging to callback_executions.jsonl; ThreadPoolExecutor removed from ops.py; lifecycle events tick_started, cron_fire, fire_decided, subprocess_started, subprocess_completed, fire_result, tick_finished.
Step 5 (6 tests PASSED): callback retry on transient failure; execution logging to scheduled_tasks.json; /api/ops/trigger-history endpoint; full lifecycle with autonomy_level="operational" and outcome="completed".
Gaps: no real-deployment end-to-end trigger test; no circuit breaker, alerting, or metrics dashboard; no load or stress tests; no DLQ or compensation logic; SOLO_CALLBACK_BASE_URL unvalidated, no rate limiting, token not vaulted; daemon crash recovery and graceful shutdown untested; no lifecycle state docs; scheduled_tasks.json fully in memory, JSONL history unbounded, file-based persistence only.
Totals: 186 tests passing, 4 trigger types, ~580 lines of duplication removed. Production readiness: ~70%.
B: E2E test: trigger creation → CloudWatch fire → callback invocation → state update (Missing). 175 tests PASSED (Step 1). 25 tests PASSED (Step 2). 18 + 69 tests PASSED (Step 3). 6 acceptance checks PASSED (Step 4). 6 tests PASSED (Step 5). 186 tests passing. 4 trigger types registered and firing. Callback with exponential backoff and detailed logging. History persisted to JSONL. Daemon integration. 580 lines duplicated, type hints validated.

107	@dataclass(frozen=True)
108	class ScheduledBlock:
109	    """An action block with a cron schedule, paired with its block dir."""
110	
111	    block: Block
112	    block_dir: Path
113	    cron: str
114	    timeout_seconds: int
115	
116	
117	def discover_scheduled_blocks(blocks_root: Path) -> list[ScheduledBlock]:
118	    """Walk ``<blocks_root>/actions/*/block.yaml`` for scheduled blocks.
119	
120	    Skips manifests that fail to parse, that are not action blocks, or
121	    that do not declare ``metadata.schedule.cron``. Returns an empty list
122	    when the actions tree does not exist — callers tolerate empty
123	    blocks roots.
124	    """
125	    out: list[ScheduledBlock] = []
126	    actions_dir = blocks_root / "actions"
127	    if not actions_dir.is_dir():
128	        return out
129	    for child in sorted(actions_dir.iterdir()):
130	        if not child.is_dir():
131	            continue
132	        manifest_path = child / "block.yaml"
133	        if not manifest_path.is_file():
134	            continue
135	        try:
136	            doc = yaml.safe_load(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
137	        except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError):
138	            continue
A: ScheduledBlock: block, block_dir, cron, timeout_seconds. discover_scheduled_blocks(blocks_root) walks <blocks_root>/actions/*/block.yaml for scheduled blocks, skipping manifests that fail to parse, are not action blocks, or lack metadata.schedule.cron. Returns empty list when actions tree doesn't exist. ParsedCron: trigger, minute, hour, day, month, day_of_week. parse_cron_expression(cron) builds a CronTrigger from 5- or 6-field cron, returns None on ValueError. build_trigger(cron) expects 5 or 6 whitespace-separated fields. is_due(trigger, *, now, lookback_seconds) returns True iff the trigger's next fire time is in (now-lookback_seconds, now]. resolve_argv(argv, *, block_dir) substitutes ${BLOCK_DIR} and ${PYTHON}. FireResult: business_id, block_id, outcome, task_id, gate_id, exit_code, duration_s, error.
B:
Listing, lines 107 to 286:
ScheduledBlock dataclass (107): block: Block, block_dir: Path, cron: str, timeout_seconds: int.
discover_scheduled_blocks(blocks_root) (117): walks <blocks_root>/actions/*/block.yaml sorted; skips parse failures, non-dict docs, Block.model_validate failures, spec.type != "action", missing metadata.schedule; returns [] when actions dir absent.
ParsedCron dataclass (161): trigger plus minute, hour, day, month, day_of_week; from_trigger() extracts field values from a CronTrigger.
parse_cron_expression(cron) (186) -> ParsedCron | None (None on ValueError).
build_trigger(cron) (200): 6 fields = second minute hour day month dow, the test extension for sub-minute firing; 5 fields = CronTrigger.from_crontab; else ValueError "cron expression must have 5 or 6 whitespace-separated fields".
is_due(trigger, *, now, lookback_seconds) (227): True iff a fire time falls in (now-lookback, now]; naive datetimes coerced to UTC; uses get_next_fire_time.
resolve_argv(argv, *, block_dir) (256): substitutes ${BLOCK_DIR} and ${PYTHON} (sys.executable).
FireResult frozen dataclass (272): business_id, block_id, outcome ('gated' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'watchdog_killed'), task_id, gate_id, exit_code, duration_s, error=None.

input:
Now I have enough information. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary: ops.heartbeat Implementation Analysis

### What the Heartbeat Block and ops.heartbeat API Do

**Block (blocks/actions/heartbeat/block.yaml):**
- Runs every 15 minutes (`cron: "*/15 * * * *"`)
- Role: ops (operations/monitoring)
- Spawns a Python subprocess that collects three snapshots: timestamp, pending gates count, and recent task transitions
- Times out after 60 seconds
- Autonomy default: auto-approved

**Agent (heartbeat_agent.py):**
- Reads from the per-business datastore (tasks.sqlite) to count:
  - **pending_gates**: gates with state == "PENDING" from the gate_index
  - **recent_task_transitions_15m**: tasks whose `updated_at` falls within the last 15 minutes
  - **app_health** (optional): hits SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL via httpx to collect optional health metrics
- Writes `HEARTBEAT.md` to `<workdir>/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md` with YAML metadata + markdown sections
- Prints a one-line JSON payload to stdout that the cron lifecycle captures as `Task.result`

**API Endpoint (/api/ops/trigger-cron-block):**
- POST endpoint to manually trigger a scheduled block via remote callback
- Validates `block_id` and `tri
A: ops.heartbeat analysis. Block: cron "*/15 * * * *", role ops, 60s timeout, autonomy auto. Agent: counts pending_gates (gate_index state == "PENDING") and recent_task_transitions_15m (updated_at within last 15 minutes) from tasks.sqlite; optional app_health via SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL; writes <workdir>/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md; JSON stdout becomes Task.result. API POST /api/ops/trigger-cron-block: validates block_id + trigger_id (.claude/scheduled_tasks.json or CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN); 200 with fire results, or 400/401/500; for ops.heartbeat also write_consolidated_heartbeat() into {SOLO_TARGET}/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md. Cron: discover_scheduled_blocks() walks blocks/actions/*/block.yaml; is_due() (APScheduler, lookback 60s); fire_block() + decide() (A4 matrix) — SUPERVISED gets pending gate, OPERATIONAL runs subprocess with watchdog; daemon repeats run_tick() every 60 seconds. Tests: test_heartbeat.py 12; test_heartbeat_http.py 18 (check_app_health mocks + Starlette e2e); test_ops.py 6 (validation 400, 401; valid 200; oauth bypass); test_solo_cron.py partial (test_fire_heartbeat_block_via_cron, test_fire_metrics_review_block_via_cron). Gaps: no docs for SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL or metrics contract; untested malformed gate_index, naive updated_at timezone; no alerting on metrics; write_consolidated_heartbeat() silent no-op without SOLO_TARGET; 15-minute window hardcoded.
B:
ops.heartbeat: cron "*/15 * * * *", role ops, autonomy auto-approved, times out after 60 seconds. Agent reads tasks.sqlite: pending_gates (state == "PENDING" from gate_index), recent_task_transitions_15m (updated_at in last 15 minutes), optional app_health via SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL. Writes <workdir>/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md, prints one-line JSON to stdout. POST /api/ops/trigger-cron-block validates block_id and trigger_id (from .claude/scheduled_tasks.json or CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env); 200 on success, 400/401/500 on failure; special case: block_id == "ops.heartbeat" also calls write_consolidated_heartbeat().

scripts/solo_cron.py: discover_scheduled_blocks() walks blocks/actions/*/block.yaml, is_due() uses APScheduler CronTrigger, fire_block() creates pending task, decide() A4 autonomy matrix (SUPERVISED or OPERATIONAL), OPERATIONAL spawns subprocess with watchdog, COMPLETED/FAILED, SUPERVISED creates pending gate. run_tick() fires due blocks, daemon repeats every 60 seconds. After run_tick(), any block_id == "ops.heartbeat" calls write_consolidated_heartbeat().

Tests: test_heartbeat.py (12 tests) — manifest validation, 0/3/7 counts, e2e with real sqlite (3 PENDING gates, 4 recent tasks, empty store, SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL optional); test_heartbeat_http.py (18 tests) — check_app_health() mocking (success, HTTP error, timeout, JSON parse error, connection errors), fallback, render_heartbeat() with app_health dict, e2e with real Starlette; test_ops.py (6 tests) — empty block_id (400), missing auth (401), invalid trigger_id (401), valid trigger fires and returns 200 OK, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN bypasses trigger_id; test_solo_cron.py (partial) — test_fire_heartbeat_block_via_cron() fires OPERATIONAL, task COMPLETED, HEARTBEAT.md written, cron events; test_fire_metrics_review_block_via_cron() seeds HEARTBEAT.md.

Gaps: no README or example config for SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL or consuming HEARTBE

A: Subscriber routes — signup, list, unsubscribe. FastAPI module. Async router: /api/subscribers. Types: SubscriberCreate(email, name: Optional[str] = None). POST "" — INSERT INTO subscriber (email, name) VALUES (:email, :name) RETURNING id, email, name, created_at; JSONResponse with id, email, name, created_at. GET "" — SELECT id, email, name, created_at FROM subscriber ORDER BY created_at DESC; JSONResponse of rows mapped to id, email, name, created_at. DELETE("/{subscriber_id}" — DELETE FROM subscriber WHERE id = :id; JSONResponse 404 if rowcount == 0 else {"ok": True}.

input:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## Comprehensive Exploration of the Cron Scheduling System

Based on my thorough analysis of the Solo platform codebase, here's a complete understanding of the cron scheduling system:

### 1. How `scripts/solo_cron.py` Works

**Purpose**: A daemon and CLI that discovers action blocks declaring `metadata.schedule.cron` and fires them at scheduled times.

**Core Architecture**:
- **Schedule Discovery** (`discover_scheduled_blocks`): Walks `blocks/actions/*/block.yaml`, parses manifests, and collects blocks with `metadata.schedule.cron` declarations
- **Cron Parsing** (`build_trigger`): Converts 5-field (standard crontab) or 6-field (test extension for sub-minute precision) cron expressions into APScheduler `CronTrigger` objects
- **Due-Window Logic** (`is_due`): Checks if a cron fire should occur "now" within a lookback window (default 60s)
- **Autonomy-Respecting Fire** (`fire_block`): Queries the autonomy matrix via `GateService.decide()` to determine if a fire should:
  - **SUPERVISED**: Create a pending gate requiring human approval
  - **OPERATIONAL** (and fallback aliases): Auto-app
A:
scripts/solo_cron.py: walks blocks/actions/*/block.yaml, parses metadata.schedule.cron, build_trigger, is_due, fire_block; run-once and daemon modes; JSON-line events. Substitutes ${PYTHON} and ${BLOCK_DIR}; subprocess cwd = manifest.workdir; task PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED|FAILED; watchdog timeout_seconds. Two modes: run-once (--fire-all ignores due-window), daemon (--tick-seconds default 60s). GateService.decide() for SUPERVISED vs OPERATIONAL. Blocks with metadata.schedule.cron declared.

blocks/actions/heartbeat/: id ops.heartbeat, name heartbeat, cron "*/15 * * * *", timeout 60s, activation onCron, argv ["${PYTHON}", "${BLOCK_DIR}/heartbeat_agent.py"], autonomy default auto. Agent reads last_checked, pending_gates, recent_task_transitions_15m via DatastoreSqlite, TaskRepository; writes <workdir>/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md; stdout JSON payload. render_heartbeat() is a pure function. Task.result stores the JSON.

.claude/scheduled_tasks.json: id 58dbe7da, block_id ops.error-scan, cron "*/30 * * * *", recurring true, durable true, created_at 2026-05-26T00:00:00Z; id 5a28925b, ops.metrics-review, "*/30 * * * *"; id 236557ba, ops.heartbeat, "*/15 * * * *", created_at 2026-05-27T10:30:00Z; id f7e4d2c1, finance.financial-review, "0 8 * * *", created_at 2026-05-27T10:45:00Z. Each entry fires the command, reports output. No imports of heartbeat_agent; remote-triggered via Anthropic SDK.

Scheduled blocks: ops.heartbeat, ops.metrics-review, ops.error-scan, finance.financial-review, ops.morning-plan, ops.backlog-groom, ops.deploy, ops.monitor, marketing.content. ops.metrics-review: cron "*/30 * * * *", timeout_seconds 60, activation onCron, argv ["${PYTHON}", "${BLOCK_DIR
B:
Solo cron scheduling, two layers.
1) /Users/rc/code/solo/scripts/solo_cron.py (725 lines): discover_scheduled_blocks walks blocks/actions/*/block.yaml for metadata.schedule.cron; build_trigger: 5-field or 6-field cron to APScheduler CronTrigger; is_due uses a lookback window (default 60s); fire_block consults GateService.decide() — SUPERVISED creates a pending gate, OPERATIONAL auto-approves and spawns an isolated subprocess with watchdog timeout. Modes: run-once (--fire-all skips due-window checks) and daemon (--tick-seconds, default 60s). JSON-line events: cron_started, fire_decided, fire_gated, subprocess_started, subprocess_completed, subprocess_failed, watchdog_killed, cron_finished. Argv substitutes ${PYTHON} and ${BLOCK_DIR}; subprocess cwd=manifest.workdir (business workdir); task lifecycle PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED or FAILED.
2) Heartbeat block at blocks/actions/heartbeat/: block.yaml has id ops.heartbeat, cron "*/15 * * * *", timeout 60s, activation onCron, argv ["${PYTHON}", "${BLOCK_DIR}/heartbeat_agent.py"], autonomy default auto. /Users/rc/code/solo/blocks/actions/heartbeat/heartbeat_agent.py (236 lines) collects last_checked, pending_gates (state == "PENDING", read from the kv index via DatastoreSqlite — does NOT import solo.core.gates), recent_task_transitions_15m (updated_at within last 15 minutes); writes <workdir>/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md (optional ## App Health when SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL set); one-line JSON on stdout, stored in Task.result.
3) /Users/rc/code/solo/.claude/scheduled_tasks.json: 4 entries, all recurring true, durable true, prompts run solo_cron.py run-once: 58dbe7da ops.error-scan */30 * * * * (2026-05-26T00:00:00Z); 5a28925b ops.metrics-review */30 * * * * (2026-05-27T10:15:00Z); 236557ba ops.heartbeat */15 * * * * (2026-05-27T10:30:00Z, newly added); f7e4d2c1 finance.financial-review 0 8 * * * (2026-05-27T10:45:00Z).
4) Blocks declaring cron: ops.heartbeat */15 * * * * (60s); ops.metrics-review */30 * * * * (60s); ops.error-scan */30 * * * * (60s); finance.financial-review 0 8 * * * (120s); ops.morning-plan 0 6 * * * (120s); ops.backlog-groom 0 7 * * 1 (120s). No schedule: ops.deploy, ops.monitor, marketing.content. Shared pattern: activation onCron, model none, autonomy default auto.

## Summary Report

I've thoroughly searched and found all the requested files related to the heartbeat agent, scheduled tasks, and cron configuration. Here's the complete inventory:

### 1. **Heartbeat Agent File**
**Path:** `/Users/rc/code/solo/blocks/workflows/heartbeat/heartbeat_agent.py`

This is the main agent file (233 lines) that:
- Runs every 15 minutes (via cron)
- Collects three operational metrics:
  1. `last_checked` - current UTC timestamp
  2. `pending_gates` - count of approval gates with `state == "PENDING"`
  3. `recent_task_transitions_15m` - count of tasks updated within the last 15 minutes
- Optionally checks app health via `SOLO_APP_HEALTH_URL` environment variable
- Writes output to `<workdir>/.solo/state/HEARTBEAT.md`
- Returns structured JSON result dict to stdout
- Has helper functions: `check_app_health()`, `count_pending_gates()`, `count_recent_task_transitions()`, `render_heartbeat()`, `collect_and_write()`

input:
Perfect! Now I have a clear picture. Let me compile a comprehensive report based on my findings:

## Report: Solo Engine Drain Loop Architecture

Based on thorough exploration of the codebase, here's how the Solo engine drain loop works:

---

### 1. **Drain Loop Discovery (queue.py & engine_tasks.py)**

**File**: `/Users/rc/code/solo/solo/core/engine/queue.py`

The drain loop discovers wakeup requests via the `WakeupRequestRepository`:

```python
class WakeupRequestRepository:
    def __init__(self, db: DatastoreSqlite) -> None:
        self._db = db
    
    def fetch_due(
        self,
        scheduled_at_lte: datetime,
        *,
        limit: int = 100,
    ) -> list[WakeupRequest]:
        # SELECT from wakeup_requests WHERE status = 'queued' AND scheduled_at <= ?
        # ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at ASC
```

The repository queries a **single SQLite database** (passed at construction) for all wakeup requests with status `QUEUED` and `scheduled_at <= now`, ordered by priority then creation time.

---

### 2. **execute_task() Pipeline (engine/__init__.py)**

**File**: `/Users/rc/code/solo/solo/core/engine/__init__.py`

The full orchestration pipeline is encapsulated in
A: Solo engine drain loop: ONE SQLite DB (platform-wide at <target>/.solo/tasks.sqlite) — not per-business. queue.py:47 WakeupRequestRepository.fetch_due(scheduled_at_lte, limit=100); engine_tasks.py:112 start_drain_loop(target, interval_s=5.0, on_tick=None); engine_tasks.py:94 _run_drain_tick(target) returns due count. BusinessRegistry.list_all() in businesses/__init__.py. Each WakeupRequest carries business_id (types.py). Drains every 5 seconds via asyncio.to_thread. No per-business polls.
B:
Drain loop architecture.
- Discovery: WakeupRequestRepository.fetch_due(scheduled_at_lte, limit=100) (queue.py:47) selects wakeup_requests where status = 'queued' and scheduled_at <= now, ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at ASC, from one SQLite DB.
- execute_task(task, session_root, ...) (engine/__init__.py:120) is the full pipeline (plan, topo-sort, drive, gate, done) via drive_step() retry wrapper; params include pre_planned_steps, escalation_handler, commit_per_step, replan_enabled, gate_service, goal_repository, max_replans=2, max_retries=3, mcp_configs, step_callback. NOT called by drain loop yet.
- WakeupRequest (types.py) fields: id, task_id, business_id, source (CRON, OPERATOR, EVENT, DEPENDENCY, CONTINUATION, RETRY), wake_reason, context_snapshot, scheduled_at, priority, status (QUEUED, CLAIMED, EXECUTED, SKIPPED, BUDGET_BLOCKED), created_at. Stored in platform-wide DB <target>/.solo/tasks.sqlite, tagged by business_id.
- BusinessRegistry.list_all() (businesses/__init__.py:218) lists manifests (id, workdir, autonomy_level) from platform DB.
- Startup: FastAPI lifespan creates start_drain_loop(target, interval_s, on_tick=None) (engine_tasks.py:112) task named solo-api-drain-loop, every DEFAULT_DRAIN_INTERVAL_S (5 seconds).
- _run_drain_tick(target) (engine_tasks.py:94): opens target/.solo/tasks.sqlite, fetch_due, marks each CLAIMED, returns count.
CRITICAL: drain loop scans ONLY the platform DB; per-business DBs (<business-workdir>/.solo/tasks.sqlite) are never scanned. Platform DB tables: businesses, wakeup_requests, tasks, gates, cost_events, kv, goals. drain_queue(repo, tick_interval_s=5.0, handler=None) (queue.py:146) exists but no handler is wired, so requests are only CLAIMED, never executed. Implications: unified cross-business queue with global priority ordering; business isolation preserved; next phase should dispatch CLAIMED requests to execute_task().

Based on my thorough examination of the cron metrics-review implementation, here's my report:

---

## Report: Metrics-Review Cron Implementation Status

### (A) What `write_consolidated_metrics_review()` Does

**Location:** `/Users/rc/code/solo/scripts/solo_cron.py` (lines 729-811)

The function aggregates metrics review data across all businesses and writes a consolidated summary:

1. **Reads per-business files**: Iterates through all registered businesses and reads their `{business_workdir}/.solo/state/METRICS_REVIEW.md` files
2. **Extracts alerts**: Parses markdown to find the "## Alerts" section and collects all alert lines (those starting with "- ")
3. **Adds business context**: Prefixes each alert with the business ID (e.g., `"business.biz1: High pending gates: 5"`)
4. **Determines status**: Sets consolidated status to "degraded" if any business has alerts, otherwise "ok"
5. **Outputs markdown**: Writes a consolidated `METRICS_REVIEW.md` to `{SOLO_TARGET}/.solo/state/METRICS_REVIEW.md` with:
   - Header with generation timestamp
   - System Status section showing
A:
write_consolidated_metrics_review() (scripts/solo_cron.py lines 729-811): reads each business .solo/state/METRICS_REVIEW.md, collects "## Alerts" lines, prefixes business ID, status "degraded" if any alerts else "ok", writes consolidated METRICS_REVIEW.md to {SOLO_TARGET}/.solo/state/; skips malformed files; no-op without SOLO_TARGET. Invoked when "ops.metrics-review" fires (lines 1052-1054 run-once, 1072-1074 daemon).
Unit tests (tests/scripts/test_solo_cron.py lines 1492-1613): aggregates alerts, handles missing files, noop without target — all pass.
Integration: test_cron_trigger_metrics_review_execution() (tests/api/test_ops.py lines 1239-1305) with trigger ID "test-metrics-review-trigger-321"; TestMetricsReviewTriggerRegistration (lines 683-806).
CRITICAL BUG, block ID mismatch: block.yaml metadata.id and solo_cron.py (lines 1053, 1073) use ops.metrics-review (hyphen), but ops.py trigger_cron_block endpoint line 837 checks "ops.metrics_review" (underscore), so the conditional NEVER triggers and consolidated output is not written via the endpoint. Test lines 1319 and 1333 also send underscore.
Fix: line 837 and the two test lines to hyphen, then e2e verify. Optional: add /api/ops/metrics-review-status endpoint; document output format. ~95% complete.
B:

15	def read_max_concurrent_tasks() -> int:

A: Thread-safe engine status tracking for the dashboard API. read_max_concurrent_tasks() reads SOLO_MAX_CONCURRENT_TASKS from env, defaulting to 4. EngineStatus holds engine_enabled, started_at, shutting_down, drain_alive, cron_alive, max_concurrent_tasks (default 4), _last_drain_tick_at, _in_flight, _lock. read_max_concurrent_tasks() returns max(1, int(raw)) or 4. record_drain_tick() increments _last_drain_tick_at. increment_in_flight() increments _in_flight. decrement_in_flight() decrements _in_flight to max(0, self._in_flight - 1). to_dict() returns uptime_seconds, last_drain_tick_at, started_at, in_flight.
B: Thread-safe engine status for dashboard API. read_max_concurrent_tasks(): env SOLO_MAX_CONCURRENT_TASKS, DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENT_TASKS = 4, max(1, int(raw)), default on ValueError. @dataclass EngineStatus: engine_enabled, started_at, shutting_down, drain_alive, cron_alive, max_concurrent_tasks; private _last_drain_tick_at, _in_flight, threading.Lock. record_drain_tick(), increment_in_flight(), decrement_in_flight() (floor 0), to_dict() → engine_enabled, shutting_down, drain_alive, cron_alive, last_drain_tick_at, started_at, uptime_seconds, max_concurrent_tasks, in_flight.
